title: Sub-workflow returns multiple branches instead of single output
symptom: When running a sub-workflow in 'Run once for each item' mode, the output is split into multiple branches, making it difficult to connect to subsequent nodes.
applies_when: Running in 'Run once for each item' mode
not_this_if: Using 'Run once with all items' mode
Sub-workflow can handle multiple items at once
root_cause: In 'Run once for each item' mode, each execution of the sub-workflow returns its result as a separate output, leading to multiple branches in the workflow. This behavior is expected and can complicate the workflow structure.
fix_steps: Open the Execute Workflow node settings.
Change the mode from 'Run once for each item' to 'Run once with all items'.
Save the workflow and test the execution.
agent_instruction: Switch the Execute Workflow node to 'Run once with all items' mode for a single aggregated output.
